summary: Completed testing of iToke voucher system with backup codes. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend testing confirmed backup codes are prominently displayed in 'Meus QR' tab. The critical MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in /api/qr/validate has been fixed.

ui_bugs.issue: Footer info bar ('QR Codes não utilizados expiram em 6 meses') intercepts click events on QR cards, making it difficult to open fullscreen view

passed_tests: POST /api/qr/generate - returns backup_code in ITK-XXX format
POST /api/qr/generate - response is JSON serializable (no ObjectId)
POST /api/qr/validate - validates by backup_code without 500 error
POST /api/qr/validate - returns 400 for already used voucher
GET /api/vouchers/my - returns client vouchers with backup_code
GET /api/vouchers/my - includes offer and establishment info
Sales records contain credits_used and amount_to_pay_cash
Frontend: 'Meus QR' tab displays vouchers with backup codes prominently
Frontend: Backup codes shown in yellow/amber bar with 'Código de Resgate' label
Frontend: Filter tabs (Todos, Gerados, Utilizados, Expirados) working
Frontend: Establishment validate screen accessible with camera and manual input modes
context_for_next_testing_agent: All critical bugs from iteration_9 have been fixed. MongoDB ObjectId serialization issue in /api/qr/validate is resolved. Frontend getMyQRCodes() now correctly calls /vouchers/my. Backup codes (ITK-XXX format) are prominently displayed in both qr.tsx cards and qr-fullscreen.tsx. The only minor issue is a footer overlay that can intercept clicks on QR cards.

verified_fixes.issue: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error in /api/qr/validate (500 error)
Backup code not displayed in 'Meus QR' tab
verified_fixes.verification: Frontend shows ITK-XXX codes in yellow/amber bar with 'Código de Resgate' label

verified_fixes.issue: QR codes not saved in 'Meus QR' tab
verified_fixes.verification: GET /api/vouchers/my returns vouchers, frontend displays them correctly
